full_content: Deliberation Archive record DELIB-0877 version 7: owner requested a careful critique of the full GT-KB/application separation problem space with adoption aligned to industry norms and conventions. The updated planning record adds a Critical Deliberation section that decomposes the problem into artifact authority, workspace/repository boundary, host/container/development-environment boundary, service/API boundary, control-plane reconciliation, and subject-scoped review/release/test boundaries; it identifies risks of over-novel terminology, treating project roots as security boundaries, privileged dashboard shells, canonical Markdown mutation, raw database credentials, over-projection, mixed roots, and CI/container escape hatches; and it recommends least privilege, workspace trust, generated configuration, policy-as-code, controller reconciliation, provenance, CODEOWNERS-style ownership, and subject-scoped CI.
explicit_change_request: Owner requested a careful critique of the entire GT-KB/application separation problem space, emphasizing alignment with industry norms and conventions and avoiding novelty that would inhibit adoption.
change_reason: Update the GT-KB/application isolation phased planning record with a critical industry-alignment deliberation and carry that critique into the top priority backlog.
